channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Don’t expect hurricane ‘truth telling from anybody’: Bill O’Reilly
item.description: "Republicans and Donald Trump are trying to use a storm for political advantage, but so is Kamala Harris,” O'Reilly said.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Cameron CH Miller discusses his vision for Las Vegas City Council Ward 5
item.description: Cameron CH Miller, a Democratic state assemblyman and candidate for Las Vegas City Council, shared his plans and priorities for Ward 5 on 8 News Now’s Daily Voting Guide.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Candidate Kamilah Bywaters discusses her vision for the Clark County School Board
item.description: Kamilah Bywaters, a candidate for the Clark County School District (CCSD) Board of Trustees, joined 8 News Now’s Daily Voting Guide to discuss her plans and priorities for District E, which covers Summerlin and northwest parts of the Las Vegas Valley.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: VP Harris covers mouth, says ‘it’s a live broadcast’ after stumbling through Hurricane Milton speech
item.description: During a meeting with officials on Hurricane Milton, Vice President Harris stumbled through a speech and later covered her mouth and said, "It's a live broadcast.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mayor Frey vetoes carbon emission fee adopted by city council
item.description: In his veto letter Minneapolis Mayor Jacob Frey said he agrees with the aims of the fee on polluters, but he said the council’s action amounts to an “unauthorized tax.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Thamer, MPR News

item.title: This is FEMA's role in preparing for Hurricane Milton
item.description: Milton is set to become the second hurricane to strike Florida in two weeks, and the Federal Emergency Management Agency is once again positioning supplies and personnel where they’re needed and coordinating with state and local officials.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Santana, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Harris risks losing crucial battleground state due to Biden's gas export pause, as Dems plead to change course
item.description: Kamala Harris is facing a growing chorus of bipartisan pressure to undo Biden's LNG pause ahead of Election Day, or risk her chances in the must-win swing state of Pennsylvania.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Vance’s Midwestern roots appeal to Michigan swing-county voters despite Buckeye ties
item.description: Ohio Sen. JD Vance can help former President Donald Trump win Michigan thanks to his Midwestern upbringing, which will appeal to voters in the critical swing state.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The presidential campaigns have made over 200 visits but speak to just a fraction of American voters
item.description: The nation's unique Electoral College system for electing a president, which replaces the popular vote, puts disproportionate voting power in the hands of a relative few states.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Afghan national charged with Election Day terror plot reignites vetting concerns: ‘Glaring alarms’
item.description: The arrest of an Afghan national, who came to the U.S. after the withdrawal from Afghanistan in 2021, on terror charges is refueling concerns about the vetting of those who arrived.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Colorado Supreme Court dismisses lawsuit against Christian baker who refused to bake trans cake
item.description: The Colorado Supreme Court dismissed another lawsuit against Masterpiece Cakeshop's Jack Phillips, who faced accusations of discrimination for refusing to bake a custom cake.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Melania Trump waves off 'hand-swatting incident': A 'mere misunderstanding'
item.description: The then-first lady made headlines in 2017 when footage taken during a trip to Tel Aviv appeared to show her brushing away Trump's hand.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, NewsNation

item.title: Former Sen. Tim Johnson, the last Democrat to hold statewide office in South Dakota, dies at 77
item.description: SIOUX FALLS, S.D. (AP) — Former Sen. Tim Johnson, a centrist who was the last Democrat to hold statewide office in South Dakota and who was adept at securing federal funding for projects back home during his nearly three decades in Washington, has died. He was 77.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump has 9-point lead on most important issue to voters: poll
item.description: A majority of voters say the economy is the most important issue going into the presidential election, and former President Trump holds a 9-point lead over Vice President Kamala Harris on the topic.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Florida Republican suggests Greene have ‘head examined’ over hurricane claims
item.description: Florida Republican Rep. Carlos A. Gimenez suggested Wednesday that Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene (R-Ga.) should have her “head examined” after she claimed humans can control the weather.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Woodward book reveals Trump's calls with Putin and Biden's private remarks on Obama and Netanyahu
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump has had as many as seven private phone calls with Vladimir Putin since leaving office and secretly sent the Russian president COVID-19 test machines during the height of the pandemic, Bob Woodward reported in his new book, “War.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE and MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: In battleground states, it's not just about winning — but also losing by less
item.description: In a state where every vote matters, both Democratic and Republican campaigns are not only trying to win in counties where they’re strongest, they’re also trying to lose by less.
item.dc:creator: Ari Shapiro, Mia Venkat, Karen Zamora, and Ashley Brown, MPR News

item.title: Fight to control NJ child care costs continues
item.description: HACKENSACK, N.J. (PIX11) - Elizabeth Velazquez is a mother to three, two of whom go to Little Learners Child Development Center in Hackensack. "My children mean the world to me," she said, holding back tears, "and I want to be able to continue to work hard every day to provide the world to them.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: Trump secretly sent Putin COVID-19 tests during pandemic shortage, a new book reports
item.description: Vice President Harris is criticizing Trump over the reported shipment, which is detailed in a new book by the journalist Bob Woodward. The president's campaign has denied the reporting.
item.dc:creator: Jason Breslow, MPR News

item.title: Harris proposes expanding Medicare to cover in-home senior care
item.description: Appearing Tuesday on ABC's “The View,” Harris talked about taking care of her mother when she was dying and personally experiencing the challenges many families face when seeking affordable in-home care for their aging loved ones.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Dolton Mayor Tiffany Henyard walks out as trustees address budget deficit
item.description: "I just want to come here and lay the law down as it relates to what's going on in our village, where we are, and where we're going," Dolton Mayor Tiffany Henyard said.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, NewsNation

item.title: Conservative senator lays out proposals for post-McConnell reforms
item.description: Sen. Mike Lee (R-Utah), the chair of the conservative Senate Steering Committee, has laid out a list of proposed reforms designed to ensure that the next Senate Republican leader does not have the same power as outgoing Senate GOP Leader Mitch McConnell (Ky.).
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: How is Mayor Adams connected to the latest federal indictment?
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- In charging Mohamed Bahi with witness tampering and destroying records, federal authorities have shed light on the separate case against Mayor Eric Adams.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: DeSantis pushes back on 'delusional' Harris remark about Hurricane Milton calls
item.description: Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis (R) said Monday that it was “delusional” for Vice President Kamala Harris to accuse him of “political gamesmanship” after reports emerged that he did not take the Democratic presidential nominee’s calls as his state braces for Hurricane Milton.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Poll: In the midst of a tight presidential race, Trump draws support from voters disillusioned with democracy
item.description: New polling shows this year’s Harris v. Trump presidential race is too close to call, with Trump drawing support from voters who are disillusioned with democracy. Results from the McCourtney Institute for Democracy’s Sept. 23-27, 2024 Mood of the Nation Poll.
item.dc:creator: Eric Plutzer and Craig Helmstetter, MPR News

item.title: North Korea's Kim again threatens to use nuclear weapons against South Korea and US
item.description: The latest warning came as experts say North Korea could ramp up hostilities ahead of the U.S. presidential election.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Rafael Arroyo discusses wasteful spending, studio tax credits, and California's policies in Nevada
item.description: Rafael Arroyo, the Republican candidate running for Assembly District 41, joined Brian Loftis on the 8 News Now Daily Voting Guide, outlining his vision and priorities as the election approaches.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump mass deportation plan embraced by some border sheriffs
item.description: Speaking exclusively to NewsNation last week, Trump detailed why he would utilize the expertise of local authorities for his deportation plans.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court won't hear arguments in Texas emergency abortion case
item.description: The Supreme Court decided not to hear arguments in a case involving Texas that could have provided an answer about whether a state abortion ban conflicts with a federal emergency care law.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, Fox 5

item.title: Minnesota Republicans seek a reset after two years of DFL trifecta in St. Paul
item.description: All 134 Minnesota House seats are on the ballot this fall, along with one state Senate seat. The results could flip political control of the Legislature.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Thousands join pro-Palestinian rallies around the globe as Oct. 7 anniversary nears
item.description: Police used tear gas and water cannons to disperse violent demonstrators in Rome as tens of thousands pro-Palestinian protesters took to the streets in European cities and around the globe, calling for a cease-fire as the first anniversary of the Hamas attacks on Israel approached.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Ballots mailed out to registered voters for Presidential General Election
item.description: Registered voters in San Diego County will be receiving their ballots next week for the November Presidential General Election.
item.dc:creator: Domenick Candelieri, Fox 5

item.title: Harris, Trump campaigns send surrogates to Las Vegas to court middle class, Latinos
item.description: With a month until Election Day, Vice President Kamala Harris and former President Donald Trump sent their surrogates to Las Vegas this week to campaign for Nevada’s six electoral votes.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Craig, Teirab tangle over Social Security, abortion in first head-to-head 2nd District debate
item.description: In a policy driven debate, candidates in Minnesota’s 2nd Congressional District part on fiscal and abortion policy while finding common ground on response to Jan. 6 Capitol uprising.
item.dc:creator: Ellie Roth and Mark Zdechlik, MPR News

item.title: Walz on Tiananmen Square screw-up: 'I'm a knucklehead at times'
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D), pressed at the vice presidential debate over false statements he made in which he said he was present during the Tiananmen Square protests in 1989, said: “I’m a knucklehead at times.
item.dc:creator: Ian Swanson, Fox 5
